rulings.Case Title: KENDRA TOMCIK DR et al VS. SIDNEY FERNALD DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Otion Of Pltfs To Compel The Depositions Of Andrew Nere, Nora Nere, And Jan Jamison And An Extension Of The Discovery Cut-Off Date To Effectuate Said Depositions; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deponents And Their Counsel Of Record Jeffrey Lewin;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 4, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFFS KENDRA TOMCIK AND CONNIE TOMCIK'S Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Andrew Nere, Nora Nere, And Jan Jamison And An Extension Of The Discovery Cut-Off Date To Effectuate Said Depositions; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deponents And Their Counsel Of Record Jeffrey Lewin. GRANTED. DEPOSITIONS MUST BE COMPLETED BY JANUARY 14, 2011. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. MOVING PARTY DID NOT MEET AND CONFER BEFORE BRINGING THIS MOTION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EVANS NATIONAL LEASING, INC. VS. KENDRA TOMCIK DR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 4, 2011, line 8, PLAINTIFF EVANS NATIONAL LEASING, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS AN ASSIGNEE OF ILS AND THE LEASE CONTAINS AN ENFORCEABLE WAIVER OF DEFENSES AGAINST AN ASSIGNEE CLAUSE. (CAL COM CODE SECTION 9403.) DEFENDANT FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. THE LEASE IS A STATUTORY FINANCE LEASE AS DEFINED BY THE CALIFORNIA UNIFORM COMMERCIAL CODE AND IS IRREVOCABLE AND INDEPENDENT UPON LESSEE'S ACCEPTANCE OF THE GOODS. (CAL COM CODE SECTION 10407.) DEFENDANT ACCEPTED THE EQUIPMENT, AND ACKNOWLEDGED ITS SATISFACTORY CONDITION AND LACK OF WARRANTIES (LEASE PARAGRAPH 3, CAL COM CODE SECTIONS 10407, 10515, 10516.) SHE MADE PAYMENTS ON THE CONTRACT AND DEFAULTED MONTHS AFTER EXECUTION. EVEN IF PLAINTIFF WAS MISLED ABOUT WHAT THE CONTRACT IN FACT COVERED, THERE WAS NO FRAUD IN THE EXECUTION BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAD A REASONABLE OPPORTUNITY TO LEARN THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT, SHE NOTICED THE ABSENCE OF SOME TERMS, AND WAS AWARE OF THE ACTUAL CHARACTER OF WHAT SHE WAS SIGNING. AS A FINANCE LENDER, AN INTEREST RATE OF GREATER THAN 10% IS ALLOWABLE. (FINANCE CODE SECTION 22002.) THE REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LAND/HOME FINANCIAL SERVICES, A CALIFORNIA VS. WARREN ROSENDALE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 4, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT STEWART TITLE GUARANTY COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. OVERRULED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LAND/HOME FINANCIAL SERVICES, A CALIFORNIA VS. WARREN ROSENDALE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 4, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT STEWART TITLE GUARANTY COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST UNAMBIGUOUSLY PLEAD AGAINST WHOM HE IS SEEKING PUNITIVE AND EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF MUST ALSO STATE FACTS SHOWING THAT ATTORNEY FEES ARE WARRANTED OR ARE LIKELY TO BE WARRANTED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: REGINALD JEAN-BAPTISTE VS. CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 4, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY'S Motion To Dismiss. GRANTED. ON 3/25/10 DEFENDANT REJECTED PLAINTIFF'S LATE CLAIM APPLICATION AND HE HAD SIX MONTHS FROM THAT DATE TO SEEK RELIEF FROM THE COURT. IN THE REJECTION LETTER, DEFENDANT WARNED PLAINTIFF THAT HE NEEDED TO FILE A PETITION FOR RELIEF WITH THE COURT. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CALHOUN BROTHERS GRADING & PAVING, INC. VS. AGNOTTI & REILLY INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 4, 2011, line 4, PLAINTIFF CALHOUN BROTHERS GRADING & PAVING, INC.'S Motion To File Second Amended Complaint. DENY WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ON 9/8/10, THE COURT STAYED THIS ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION. MOVING PARTY HAS FAILED TO SEEK RELIEF FROM THAT STAY OR EXPLAIN WHY IT WOULD BE APPROPRIATE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT IN LIGHT OF THE ONGOING ARBITRATION. =(302/CWW)
